behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions (month 13, day 32, malformed syntax) with descriptive errors.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is parser validation logic at a single function/object boundary. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: mocks are unnecessary; real failure is in local parsing rules and error construction. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text is specifically about parser rejection and error message content, not cross-module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Call the parser directly with invalid cron strings and assert structured error type/message includes the specific invalid field/syntax cause.

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for standard cron expressions, including last-day-of-month and nearest-weekday cases.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior targets timestamp computation correctness (algorithmic calendar logic), which can be exercised in one calculator boundary with fixed inputs. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: using deterministic fixtures does not hide this class of failure because the computation itself is under test. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on calculator output correctness, not parser->calculator or service->repository handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke next-run calculator with fixed start times and parsed schedule objects for representative cron patterns, asserting exact next N timestamps for normal and edge calendar cases.

behaviors.description: Overlap detector flags schedules that fire within a configured minimum gap and reports conflicting windows.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure often appears at module boundaries (schedule expansion output -> overlap comparison -> conflict window reporting). Unit tests on only comparison math can miss timestamp normalization/handoff bugs. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required, so not System. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Integration-brake checks: (a) no real infra dependency, (b) no AI output dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM-stage orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser/schedule expansion with overlap detector in-process; feed two cron schedules plus a minimum-gap config and assert detected conflict intervals and boundary behavior at exactly gap-minus-1 and gap-equal thresholds.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling is correct across DST spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity for a 2:30 AM job.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failures emerge when scheduler logic interacts with timezone/DST handling components (local-time interpretation, ambiguity policy, conversion). Pure unit mocks of timezone behavior can hide real DST edge bugs. REAL-INFRA: still in-process with real timezone rules/library; no required real network/subprocess/OS event, so System is not minimum. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text is about DST correctness in scheduling logic, not real OS primitives or external services.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end in-process scheduling flow with a real timezone database/library for a DST-observing zone; assert expected next run outcomes for spring-forward nonexistent 2:30 and fall-back duplicated 2:30 according to defined policy.

behaviors.description: If computed next run is in the past, engine applies missed-run policy (skip vs backfill) correctly.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior depends on handoff between next-run computation, current-time comparison, and policy application; single-function tests can miss state/config propagation errors. REAL-INFRA: no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess; controllable clock and in-process components suffice. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Integration-brake checks all negative for System/Agentic/Workflow escalation.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise scheduling engine with injected clock, job state, and missed-run policy; simulate downtime so next run is past due, then assert whether engine emits skip or backfill actions per policy and updates schedule state correctly.
